title: cerastium vulgatum var. holosteoides
intro: Cerastium vulgatum var. holosteoides, also known as the mouse-ear chickweed, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Cerastium vulgatum var. holosteoides include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used topically to treat minor cuts and scrapes.
